steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The researchers claim caterpillar sounds are "directed primarily at predators." To support this, we need evidence that predators actually react to the sounds — not just that the sounds exist or vary.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'sound varied across species': Shows species-level variation in sounds, but tells us nothing about whether predators respond. ✗
  • Choice B — 'chickens and yellow warblers stopped attacking in response to the sounds': Two known caterpillar predators halting attacks proves the sounds function as predator deterrents — exactly what "directed at predators" requires. ✓
  • Choice C — 'each species typically produced one sound, sometimes more': Addresses within-species consistency, not predator response. ✗
  • Choice D — 'clicks were detectable by birds, whistles were not': Shows partial detectability but doesn't show that any predator responds or is deterred. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
